channel.title: Middlegame Memo
channel.description: Fresh takes for adult chess improvers.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: adult chess improvement plan
item.description: The persistent myths around adult chess improvement plan, stress-tested against what adult chess improvers actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your openings in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying openings project for adult chess improvers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/TournamentChess is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the study plans community this week.

item.title: The endgames checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified endgames purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of tactics
item.description: The most common early stumbles in tactics, and the cheap corrections that fix them for adult chess improvers.

item.title: "tactics training routine" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about tactics training routine through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your study plans questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the study plans questions adult chess improvers keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in endgames with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest endgames adjustments that adult chess improvers report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: tactics training routine
item.description: The persistent myths around tactics training routine, stress-tested against what adult chess improvers actually report.

item.description: One contained, satisfying openings project for adult chess improvers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.
